tutorial.title: Cold-Hammer Tutorial
tutorial.subtitle: Forge a cast part into a tool

objectives.label: Set up a Stone Anvil
substeps: Add a cast head or blade and a stick.
Hold a hammer and right-click to start.

objectives.label: Cold-hammer a finished tool
substeps: Right-click in the green band to land a clean strike.
The band narrows as the metal work-hardens.
Sneak-click to bank your tier, or push on for Masterwork.

pages.text: A cast head or blade is rough. Set it on the &6Stone Anvil&r with a stick, hold a hammer, and &6cold-hammer&r it into a finished tool. A sweeping cursor crosses a band — right-click in the &agreen&r to land a good strike. Each clean hit hardens the metal and the band narrows and speeds up.

Quality climbs with good strikes: &7Crude&r, &fStandard&r, &eFine&r, &6Masterwork&r. Quality drives the tool's durability, effectiveness, and a chance at a special perk. &6Sneak-click&r to stop and bank the current tier — safe.

But a miss on hardened metal &ccracks&r it and drops a tier. If a piece is getting brittle, carry it back to a &6Bloomery&r and hold the &6anneal&r band: annealing resets the metal to wide-and-slow without losing the hardness you earned. Careful smiths anneal to keep climbing safely.

pages.caption: Anvil, cast blade, haft, finished sword
